evals.description: Scope elicitation — produce scope summary from text feature description
evals.prompt: Preciso criar uma feature de autenticação via JWT para a API. Os usuários deverão fazer login com email e senha e receber um token JWT válido por 24 horas. Produza agora o resumo de escopo da Fase 1 para esta feature, com todos os elementos: Intenção, Invariantes/Restrições, Pré-condições e Pós-condições. Se houver lacunas, inclua perguntas de elicitação logo após o resumo.
assertions: The output presents a consolidated scope summary containing all four elements: Intent, Invariants or Constraints, Pre-conditions, and Post-conditions
The output explicitly asks the user for confirmation or approval (Gate 1) before proceeding to Phase 2 or creating any file
The output does NOT create, write, or propose the content of a PLAN.md file
The scope summary references JWT or token authentication in the Intent or Post-conditions section

evals.description: Language compliance — full response in pt-BR when user prompt is in Portuguese
evals.prompt: Quero criar um endpoint REST para listar todos os usuários com paginação (page e limit via query params). Execute a Fase 1 do spec-plan e me mostre o resumo de escopo.
assertions: The entire response is written in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR), not English
The response includes a scope summary with at least Pre-conditions and Post-conditions sections
The scope summary mentions paginação, page, or limit as part of the post-conditions or constraints
The output does NOT generate or propose PLAN.md file content without first obtaining user approval

evals.description: Plan schema compliance — semantic task IDs and one file per task
evals.prompt: O escopo foi aprovado para a feature 'Alterar senha do usuário' no módulo user. Pré-condição: usuário autenticado com token JWT válido. Pós-condição: senha atualizada no banco de dados, resposta HTTP 204. Invariante: sem novas bibliotecas externas, sem migrações de banco de dados. Gere agora o plano técnico completo seguindo estritamente o schema: (1) Seção 1 — Roadmap com tasks em formato TASK-[LAYER]-[CONCERN], cada uma com campo 'File:' e sub-tasks em '[ ]'; (2) Seção 3 — Execution Checklist espelhando cada task do Roadmap com campos Agent Notes, Files Modified e Validation Status.
assertions: The plan uses semantic task IDs following the TASK-[LAYER]-[CONCERN] pattern (e.g., TASK-DOM-PASSWORD, TASK-APP-CHANGE-PASSWORD, TASK-INFRA-REPO)
Each task in the plan references exactly one file in the 'File:' field — no task spans multiple files
The plan includes an Execution section (Section 3) that mirrors every task defined in the Plan section
The plan uses markdown checkbox format [ ] for every task and sub-task entry

evals.description: No code generation — skill must not produce Go source code
evals.prompt: Crie um plano técnico para adicionar um campo 'phone' na entidade User do módulo user da nossa API hexagonal em Go.
assertions: The output does NOT include Go source code (no 'func ', 'type struct', or ':= ' patterns inside code blocks)
The response addresses Phase 1 scope elicitation before jumping to create a plan or write any file
The response stays within the planning domain — describing what tasks need to be done, not how to implement them in code

evals.description: Multi-layer decomposition — plan tasks span at least two DDD layers
evals.prompt: Escopo aprovado: adicionar soft delete na entidade Order do módulo order. O usuário cancela um pedido via POST /orders/{id}/cancel, o status muda para CANCELLED. Nenhuma migração de banco de dados é permitida. Gere o plano técnico completo.
assertions: The plan contains tasks from at least two distinct architectural layers (DOM, APP, INFRA, or MOD)
The plan includes at least one Domain layer task (TASK-DOM-*) related to the Order entity or the CANCELLED status
The plan includes at least one Infrastructure or Application layer task for the HTTP handler, repository, or command handler
The plan does NOT include any database migration task, respecting the no-migration invariant

evals.description: PRD file path — skill reads the file rather than inventing its content
evals.prompt: Tenho um PRD localizado em docs/order/cancel-order/PRD.md. Como você iniciaria o processo de spec-plan para esta feature?
assertions: The response mentions reading or loading the PRD file at the path docs/order/cancel-order/PRD.md
The response does NOT invent or fabricate PRD content (acceptance criteria, requirements) without having read the file
The response describes Phase 1 scope elicitation as the first step to be executed after reading the file
The response does NOT produce a complete PLAN.md content without first reading the PRD and validating the scope

evals.description: Constraint extraction — no redundant questions when all info is already in the prompt
evals.prompt: Feature: endpoint GET /users/{id}/profile que retorna dados públicos do usuário. Pré-condição: o usuário deve existir no banco de dados. Pós-condição: retorna HTTP 200 com JSON contendo os campos name, email e avatar_url. Invariante: sem novas dependências externas e sem migrações de banco de dados. Produza o resumo de escopo da Fase 1.
assertions: The scope summary correctly includes the pre-condition about user existence in the database
The scope summary correctly includes the post-condition about HTTP 200 with name, email, and avatar_url fields
The scope summary correctly includes the invariant about no new external dependencies and no database migrations
The output does NOT ask questions about information already explicitly provided in the prompt (pre-conditions, post-conditions, and invariants are all given)

evals.description: Phase gate enforcement — Phase 2 must not start without explicit user approval
evals.prompt: Feature: criar um domínio de notificações com suporte a notificações por email. O sistema deve enviar um email de boas-vindas quando um novo usuário é criado. Inicie o spec-plan.
assertions: The response produces a Phase 1 scope summary and explicitly waits for user confirmation before advancing
The response does NOT jump directly to creating a PLAN.md or an Execution Roadmap without Gate 1 approval
The scope summary identifies at least one invariant or constraint relevant to the notification domain (e.g., integration with user creation, no external email library assumptions)
The response ends with a clear request for the user to confirm, approve, or continue
